channel.title: Design of Master on YiSheng's secret garden
channel.description: Recent content in Design of Master on YiSheng's secret garden

item.title: May 16th — A Midsummer Day in Douliu
item.description:
slug: writting

Regressing to a Primitive State

While doing my literature review, I realized my writing and argumentation had degraded to the point where I needed to type out fragmented notes and ask AI to assemble them into sentences for me. I would read them, understand them, then go back and revise.

item.title: Senior Lab Mates Share Their Design School Experience
item.description:
slug: exp-senior-design

The Technical Crossroads

Today I asked my senior lab mates: “I feel stuck at a crossroads — one step back is device interaction, one step forward is artificial intelligence. How did you figure out which of the thousands of papers you actually wanted to study?”

item.title: Graduate Tools — Tips from Experience
item.description:
slug: exp-seminar

You can find past papers from a conference you’re interested in, right-click and print the page as a PDF for a bunch of paper titles, then give it to Claude to see if any match your topic. You can also ask Perplexity to search for related ones. Otherwise, look for papers with similar problem statements.

You can first use Gemini to extract summary keywords — 10 from an engineering perspective and 10 from a design perspective, then organize the most important ones. After the analysis, give it the full text for a more accurate result. For the filtered papers, you don’t have to translate them one by one yourself.

item.title: Ivan Said — No Two Leaves in the World Are Exactly the Same
item.description:
slug: stay-alive

The Birth of Life

Once a year, pink coral spawns on the weekend — 05.09.26. Late at night, I saw this news in the group chat and a thought suddenly came to me:

I want to go witness the birth of life, because it is truly beautiful.

item.title: Things I Learned as Staff at an International Conference
item.description:
Do Your Best, and Always Ask: “Is There Anything I Can Help With?”

These are the things I learned:

  • Actively participate in the conference and offer timely assistance to increase interaction and visibility
  • Always carry your laptop and stay present in the office to show commitment and professionalism
  • Build familiarity through consistent attendance and daily conversations, laying the groundwork for future collaboration
  • Prepare business cards to help expand your network
  • Even when your contribution seems limited, keep showing up — trust accumulates over time
  • Stay open to asking questions and learning from others
  • Have regular weekly check-ins with your mentor to maintain a steady feedback loop

item.title: When Discipline Becomes Routine: Preparing for CAADRIA Two Months Out, I Chose to Sync with the World
item.description:
slug: prep-CAADRIA-2026

When Discipline Becomes Routine: Preparing for CAADRIA Two Months Out, I Chose to Sync with the World

Translation Is Not Enough

Preparatory study to reduce the cognitive gap. Many people say, “Just use body language or a translation app to chat.” But having managed cross-national team collaboration before, I know that is far from enough. Pronunciation, regional language isolation (different countries have different vocabulary preferences — even the same language can be incomprehensible), and beyond that, one-to-one translation wastes so much time.

item.title: After Experiencing the Silent Darkness of the Deep Sea, I Realized What the Meaning of Life Is!
item.description:
slug: 0421

After Experiencing the Silent Darkness of the Deep Sea, I Realized What the Meaning of Life Is!

Before the boat dive, I couldn’t quite feel it — Why am I here? What am I supposed to be doing here? Why am I confining myself to NYUST?

item.title: Writing My Own Story
item.description:
slug: Story

Writing My Own Story

I believe life is less about finding yourself and more about creating yourself. At 20, I transitioned from seeing life through a lens of limitation to embracing the role of the protagonist in my own journey. This shift sparked a decade of entrepreneurship, international collaboration, and a relentless pursuit of excellence.

item.title: The 22nd International conference on Cooperative Design Visualization and Engineering
item.description:
From Cooperation to Emotion: Reflections on CDVE 2025

The 22nd International Conference on Cooperative Design, Visualization, and Engineering (CDVE 2025) will take place in Bangkok, Thailand, from October 19–22, 2025.
This year’s theme centers on “cooperation” — not only between systems, but between people, ideas, and disciplines.
